coverage.framework_detected: Spring Boot 2.7.0, Java 11, spring-boot-starter-web, security, data-jpa, validation, H2
coverage.notes: Maven single-module app; controllers, services, JPA repository, entities under com.example.moodsense.demo

api_design.check: POST uploadMood returns void without explicit HTTP semantics
api_design.detail: uploadMood is void and does not return ResponseEntity or 201 Created; clients lack a consistent JSON envelope or problem details for errors.

api_design.check: CrudRepository ID type does not match Mood primary key
api_design.detail: MoodRepository extends CrudRepository<Mood, String> but Mood uses int @GeneratedValue id; ID typing is inconsistent and error-prone for callers.

domain_structure.detail: Packages split into controller, service, repository (spelled repositary), model, and config; responsibilities mostly aligned with layers.
Package name typo repositary reduces clarity versus standard repository spelling.

business_logic.detail: getBestLocationFromCoordinate uses location.getLatitude() - coordinate.getLongitude() for the second squared term instead of differencing longitudes; nearby matching is mathematically wrong.
For each happy coordinate the code loads all Location rows and scans linearly; no spatial index or bounded query.

data_access.detail: JPQL uses :remoteUser @Param for mood distribution and happy coordinates, reducing injection risk and enforcing per-user filtering.
application.properties enables H2 in-memory database and h2-console; fine for demo but not production persistence or ops posture.

authentication.detail: SpringSecurityConfig defines user1/pwd1 and user2/pwd2 in source; no external IdP, rotation, or secret management.
httpBasic().and().csrf().disable() disables CSRF for all endpoints; mitigated somewhat by stateless Basic auth but still a broad relaxation.
Controller uses request.getRemoteUser() when saving and querying moods so data is tied to authenticated user.

validation.detail: latitude and longitude use @NotNull @Min @Max with clear messages for demo bounds.
Mood is not annotated with @RequestBody or validated with @Valid; servlet parameter binding may not run full Bean Validation on Mood while query params use @Validated.

error_handling.detail: No @ControllerAdvice or RFC7807-style problem responses; clients get default Spring error shapes and inconsistent behavior on validation failures.
void controller method gives no explicit success payload or location header; harder for API consumers to handle outcomes uniformly.

gaps.check: CrudRepository ID type does not match Mood primary key
gaps.detail: MoodRepository extends CrudRepository<Mood, String> but Mood uses int @GeneratedValue id; ID typing is inconsistent and error-prone for callers.
getBestLocationFromCoordinate uses location.getLatitude() - coordinate.getLongitude() for the second squared term instead of differencing longitudes; nearby matching is mathematically wrong.
SpringSecurityConfig defines user1/pwd1 and user2/pwd2 in source; no external IdP, rotation, or secret management.

For each happy coordinate the code loads all Location rows and scans linearly; no spatial index or bounded query.
